svg.title: JavaScript operator precedence tower — highest to lowest priority
svg.desc: A tower showing operator precedence from highest at top (brackets) to lowest at bottom (assignment), mirroring BODMAS from school maths

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why do brackets have the highest precedence in JavaScript?')
g.text: Brackets — always run first. Override everything.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the exponent operator ** in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('What are unary operators in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('Why do multiplication and division come before addition in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('When does addition run in JavaScript operator precedence?')
sendPrompt('When do comparison operators run in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('When do equality operators run in JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('When do logical operators run in JavaScript?')

svg.text: Same as school BODMAS: Brackets → Orders → Division/Multiplication → Addition/Subtraction → rest
